Content: [Music] Petty: So I can double-check that he put everything up.
Content_zh: [音樂] Petty：這樣我就能再確認他有把東西都放上去。

Content: The rest of them, I'm going to scan them all, yeah, so we can have them.
Content_zh: 剩下的我都會掃描起來，對，這樣我們就有檔案了。

Content: We have a, you know, our concept art directory.
Content_zh: 我們有一個概念美術資料夾。

Content: You know, in theory if all the puzzles were done and the things were decided on, we could actually make a pretty functional game with really rough art early on
Content_zh: 理論上，如果所有謎題都完成、事情也都決定好了，我們其實很早就能用很粗略的美術做出功能相當完整的遊戲，

Content: to sort of unblock the puzzle design or implementation.
Content_zh: 讓謎題設計或實作不要卡住。

Content: And some of the systems work, which would be nice.
Content_zh: 而且部分系統也能運作，那會很不錯。

Content: I'm not sure.
Content_zh: 我不確定。

Content: I'm a big fan of working that way, but I'm not sure if we'll be able to do it for this project or not.
Content_zh: 我很喜歡那種工作方式，但不確定這個專案能不能這樣做。

Content: We'll have to see how that works out.
Content_zh: 只能再看看會怎麼發展。

Content: [Music] Rice: Yep.
Content_zh: [音樂] Rice：對。

Content: If you're always showing this board, won't they get spoilers from the board?
Content_zh: 如果你們一直拍這塊板子，他們不會從板子上被劇透嗎？

Content: So just to bring us up to date, I need another location for the cloud nest.
Content_zh: 所以先更新一下進度，我還需要另一個雲巢地點。

Content: Things that came up from the art jam.
Content_zh: 這些是 art jam 裡冒出來的東西。

Content: Cloud nests.
Content_zh: 雲巢。

Content: Rice: You can tell that that's going to look awesome.
Content_zh: Rice：你看得出來那會很棒。

Content: Schafer: The first thing Peter mentioned, too.
Content_zh: Schafer：這也是 Peter 第一個提到的東西。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I think he drew that the first day.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我記得他第一天就畫了那個。

Content: Schafer: And then the other one is the dead-eye pyramid.
Content_zh: Schafer：然後另一個是死眼金字塔。

Content: Rice: Why is it dead-eye?
Content_zh: Rice：為什麼叫死眼？

Content: Schafer: Spoiler. Can't tell you.
Content_zh: Schafer：劇透，不能告訴你。

Content: Rice: I have to play the game to find out?
Content_zh: Rice：我得玩遊戲才會知道嗎？

Content: Schafer: These things all still work but maybe this becomes his story.
Content_zh: Schafer：這些東西都還能用，但也許這會變成他的故事。

Content: The monster lands move over here.
Content_zh: 怪物之地移到這邊。

Content: Maybe that moves over there.
Content_zh: 也許那個移到那邊。

Content: Rice: What I still am not clear on is, and I don't know if you are, is switching back and forth between these stories
Content_zh: Rice：我還是不太清楚，不知道你是不是清楚，就是這兩條故事之間要怎麼來回切換，

Content: and how we're going to handle that.
Content_zh: 以及我們要怎麼處理。

Content: Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。

Content: I'm putting that off.
Content_zh: 我先把那個擱著。

Content: Rice: Yeah. Start thinking about it.
Content_zh: Rice：對，該開始想了。

Content: So I'm just wondering then, like if I'm here, here and here and I jump back to this, then what's the world like?
Content_zh: 所以我就在想，如果我在這裡、這裡和這裡，然後跳回這個，那這個世界會是什麼狀態？

Content: Like why can't I do this and what happens that makes me so I can do this?
Content_zh: 像是我為什麼不能這麼做？又發生了什麼，讓我可以這麼做？

Content: Schafer: I don't know. I don't know yet.
Content_zh: Schafer：我不知道，還不知道。

Content: Schafer: I've been a little behind.
Content_zh: Schafer：我有點落後了。

Content: It's frustrating because I, when the team is waiting for you, that's always frustrating, so.
Content_zh: 這很讓人挫折，因為當整個團隊都在等你的時候，總是很挫折，所以……

Content: It's always been that way.
Content_zh: 一直都是這樣。

Content: Like having an absolutely terrifying deadline is the only thing that, makes me get it done.
Content_zh: 好像只有那種可怕到不行的期限，才會逼我把事情做完。

Content: There's just so many other things going on with the company right now that, you know, The Cave is getting to this point where it needs, you know,
Content_zh: 公司現在還有太多其他事情在進行，像《The Cave》也到了需要……

Content: it has a dialogue deadline of its own.
Content_zh: 它也有自己的對白期限。

Content: You know, and do I help with that or do I work on this game?
Content_zh: 所以我是要幫那邊，還是繼續做這款遊戲？

Content: Suck it!
Content_zh: 吃我的啦！

Content: [Monster Roar] Schafer: Peter McConnell's here today.
Content_zh: [怪物吼聲] Schafer：Peter McConnell 今天來了。

Content: It's fun to see what he's going to add to it.
Content_zh: 很期待看他會加進什麼東西。

Content: Schafer: So the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以這款遊戲。

Content: You see, we did some art already.
Content_zh: 你看，我們已經做了一些美術。

Content: We had art jam with Peter Chan and Scott Campbell and Nathan came to town and hung out with Lee, and did some art.
Content_zh: 我們和 Peter Chan、Scott Campbell 辦了 art jam，Nathan 也來城裡跟 Lee 一起做了一些美術。

Content: And that's cool.
Content_zh: 那很酷。

Content: And that corkboard is kind of up-to-date.
Content_zh: 那塊軟木板也算是更新過了。

Content: I could add some as we talk, but we're doing a point and click graphic adventure.
Content_zh: 我們邊聊我可以邊補一些上去，但我們做的是點擊式圖像冒險遊戲。

Content: I don't know if you heard that. McConnell: Very cool.
Content_zh: 不知道你有沒有聽說。McConnell：很酷。

Content: Schafer: So it doesn't have a high concept in terms of like a heavy metal fantasy world or the Day of the Dead.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以它沒有那種很明確的高概念，像重金屬奇幻世界或亡靈節之類的。

Content: It's really like a new world that we're making up based on Nathan Stapley's art.
Content_zh: 它比較像是我們根據 Nathan Stapley 的美術創造出來的新世界。

Content: You know, just having that cool look and having it be about themes that are different, maybe than other themes.
Content_zh: 就是有那種很酷的視覺，主題也會跟其他東西不太一樣。

Content: McConnell: Yeah. I've got questions.
Content_zh: McConnell：嗯，我有問題。

Content: Schafer: There'll be no questions during this meeting.
Content_zh: Schafer：這場會議禁止提問。

Content: Schafer: Is Peter McConnell excited to be on the project?
Content_zh: Schafer：Peter McConnell 對加入這個專案興奮嗎？

Content: You're asking me if he's excited?
Content_zh: 你是在問我他興不興奮嗎？

Content: I think so.
Content_zh: 我想是吧。

Content: We always enjoyed working together.
Content_zh: 我們一直很喜歡一起工作。

Content: You know, he's worked on everything I've done since Day of the Tentacle, Monkey II, Monkey II.
Content_zh: 從《Day of the Tentacle》、《Monkey Island 2》開始，我做過的東西他幾乎都有參與。

Content: Offscreen Voice: He's like your Danny Elfman.
Content_zh: 畫外音：他就像你的 Danny Elfman。

Content: Schafer: He's what?
Content_zh: Schafer：他像什麼？

Content: Offscreen Voice: He's like your Danny Elfman.
Content_zh: 畫外音：他就像你的 Danny Elfman。

Content: Schafer: He's like my Danny Elfman.
Content_zh: Schafer：他就像我的 Danny Elfman。

Content: He can do Danny Elfman if you ask him to.
Content_zh: 如果你要他做 Danny Elfman 風格，他也做得到。

Content: McConnell: I'm just thinking about instruments, you know.
Content_zh: McConnell：我只是在想樂器。

Content: I'm thinking about styles and I'm probably going to have to think quite a bit about it,
Content_zh: 我在想風格，可能得花不少時間思考，

Content: because it's, I mean, I have some things that are popping into my head.
Content_zh: 因為我是說，我腦中已經冒出一些東西。

Content: I don't know if they're probably way jumping the gun at this point.
Content_zh: 但我不知道現在想這些會不會太早。

Content: But what comes to mind about, about a world where things are being made for you and it's like a playroom a little bit, is a music box.
Content_zh: 不過想到一個什麼都替你準備好、又有點像遊戲室的世界，我腦中浮現的是音樂盒。

Content: And you know, a little bit like Mr. Roger's Neighborhood, which…
Content_zh: 還有一點像《羅傑先生的鄰居》，就是……

Content: Schafer: It also has that repetitive quality to it.
Content_zh: Schafer：它也有那種重複的特質。

Content: Because the game is going to start in this like, his routine is the same every day, even though it's drastically different.
Content_zh: 因為遊戲一開始會是這樣，他每天的例行公事都一樣，雖然內容非常不一樣。

Content: It's really like he wakes up, the robots dress him.
Content_zh: 就像他醒來，機器人替他穿衣服。

Content: You know, the arms just come out of nowhere and make him dinner, food.
Content_zh: 機械手臂突然冒出來，替他準備晚餐、食物。

Content: And then when he busts out and he's doing actual 90's, 80's creepy space adventure.
Content_zh: 等他逃出去之後，就進入真正那種八、九零年代詭異太空冒險。

Content: Or 2001 like.
Content_zh: 或者像《2001 太空漫遊》那樣。

Content: McConnell: Yeah. Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: McConnell：對。Schafer：對。

Content: McConnell: Then 2001 would be, 2001 will be hard to do, you know, without a great big orchestra.
Content_zh: McConnell：那《2001 太空漫遊》就會很難，沒有大型管弦樂團很難做。

Content: And I don't think we have that.
Content_zh: 而我想我們沒有那個。

Content: But I don't think, you know.
Content_zh: 但我想，嗯……

Content: Schafer: And now it's like a little more like now we're running our own actual budget.
Content_zh: Schafer：現在比較像是我們真的在管理自己的預算。

Content: Schafer: It's like we have a certain amount of money in the budget for Peter, which means a certain amount of time.
Content_zh: Schafer：預算裡分給 Peter 的錢就那麼多，也就代表時間就那麼多。

Content: And we have to like wait until we're ready for him to be really effective in working.
Content_zh: 所以我們得等到準備好了，讓他的工作能真正有效發揮。

Content: So we've got to be at a point where we can just tell him, here's a list of characters that need themes.
Content_zh: 我們得進展到可以直接告訴他，這是需要主題曲的角色清單。

Content: Here's a list of areas, 'cause he's got to spread out his time so he doesn't just score the first two acts and then run out of time.
Content_zh: 這是場景清單，因為他得分配好時間，不能只替前兩幕配完樂就沒時間了。

Content: So he's got to like spread it out, look at the whole game, think about the game play.
Content_zh: 所以他得把時間攤開，看完整款遊戲，思考遊戲玩法。

Content: Then think about how much of that money he should save to pay for things like live musicians.
Content_zh: 然後再想那筆錢要留多少來付現場樂手之類的費用。

Content: Studio time.
Content_zh: 還有錄音室時間。

Content: McConnell: Yeah, I think there is going to have to be a signature piece, for sure.
Content_zh: McConnell：對，我覺得一定得有一首代表性的曲子。

Content: And that is, or you know, or two halves of the signature piece because you got these two worlds.
Content_zh: 或者說，是一首代表曲的兩個半邊，因為你們有這兩個世界。

Content: Min: Yeah, yeah, that, too.
Content_zh: Min：對，對，那也是。

Content: Rice: It'll be interesting to see how we can handle that.
Content_zh: Rice：會很有趣，看我們要怎麼處理。

Content: You know, like if there're themes that persist in both their worlds or you know, funny little things to do to connect their two.
Content_zh: 像是有沒有某些主題會貫穿他們兩個世界，或是用一些有趣的小東西把兩邊連起來。

Content: Min: I think that Pete's going to be able to knock that out pretty easily.
Content_zh: Min：我覺得 Pete 應該能很輕鬆搞定。

Content: I think it's more the initial, but then it's the same thing with Tim, you know, I mean you go crazy for two days
Content_zh: 我覺得比較難的是一開始，但 Tim 也是一樣，你會先抓狂兩天，

Content: and then on the third day you just kind of come up with something.
Content_zh: 然後第三天就會想出東西。

Content: You know, that's how creative magic happens.
Content_zh: 創意魔法就是這樣發生的。

Content: I mean, and I think it's almost like you have to be patient with yourself and not, not panic.
Content_zh: 我是說，我覺得你幾乎就是得對自己有耐心，不要慌。

Content: McConnell: You know, that backer money don't last forever.
Content_zh: McConnell：贊助者的錢可不是永遠花不完。

Content: Rice: That's the truth.
Content_zh: Rice：這倒是真的。

Content: Music Lumberjack: That's not a word, is it?
Content_zh: Music Lumberjack：那不是一個字，對吧？

Content: Tree: No. We're done here.
Content_zh: Tree：不是，我們到此為止。

Content: Kipnis: Yes! Schafer: Awesome. Done. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Kipnis：太好了！Schafer：讚，完成。Rice：對。

Content: For the last four months we've spent in preproduction mode and it's been kind of exploratory phase of trying to figure out what the game is
Content_zh: 過去四個月我們都處於前期製作模式，是一個探索階段，試著弄清楚這款遊戲到底是什麼，

Content: and both in terms of design, but also in how we're going to actually make that happen.
Content_zh: 不只是在設計上，也包括我們實際上要怎麼把它做出來。

Content: But yeah, everyone's kind of had their own separate little tasks and those are all starting to converge now to where
Content_zh: 不過對，每個人原本都有各自的小任務，現在那些事情都開始匯集起來，

Content: we're ready to start actually building a puzzle in the engine.
Content_zh: 到了我們準備好在引擎裡實際做出一個謎題的階段。

Content: Crook: Actual production work is awesome.
Content_zh: Crook：真正進入製作很棒。

Content: Like we're going to go, this could get shipped.
Content_zh: 像是我們要做的東西，真的有可能被推出去。

Content: Like this work.
Content_zh: 像這些工作。

Content: Schafer: We'll see how it turns out.
Content_zh: Schafer：看看結果會怎樣。

Content: Crook: Yeah, we'll see.
Content_zh: Crook：對，看看吧。

Content: Rice: Ray Crook, Double Fine animator.
Content_zh: Rice：Ray Crook，Double Fine 動畫師。

Content: Yeah, Ray started on the project and he's been someone who's been at the company since like essentially the beginning.
Content_zh: 對，Ray 開始加入這個專案了，他基本上從公司一開始就在這裡。

Content: Crook: I was excited to work with Tim again, because Brutal Legend was the last game that,
Content_zh: Crook：我很期待再次跟 Tim 合作，因為《Brütal Legend》是上一款……

Content: you know, we'd worked closely together on.
Content_zh: 我們密切合作的遊戲。

Content: And so I was most excited just to work with him.
Content_zh: 所以我最期待的就是跟他合作。

Content: But you know, I knew there would be cameras.
Content_zh: 但我也知道會有攝影機。

Content: Crook: So I'm actually excited to be starting on that.
Content_zh: Crook：所以我其實很期待開始做那個。

Content: Crook: You know, the beginning of preproduction is really exciting and then it seems like eventually it gets kind of like,
Content_zh: Crook：前期製作一開始真的很讓人興奮，但到後來好像會變成，

Content: you're just kind of churning.
Content_zh: 有點只是在原地消耗。

Content: You know, I guess just, let's just get this show on the road, you know, is sort of the way that it feels.
Content_zh: 就會覺得，好啦，趕快開始動工吧。

Content: And that's where we are.
Content_zh: 我們現在就是到這一步了。

Content: Schafer: Awesome. Bye.
Content_zh: Schafer：太棒了，拜。

Content: Crook: Tim has picked a puzzle, which we're calling First Playable.
Content_zh: Crook：Tim 選了一個謎題，我們稱它為 First Playable。

Content: Petty: So these are some of the stuff Peter gave me on Friday, right?
Content_zh: Petty：所以這些是 Peter 星期五給我的一些東西，對吧？

Content: And so I think this is the stuff we talked about when he was here, which is the idea of them having this little sacrificial island, which is awesome.
Content_zh: 我想這就是他來的時候我們談過的東西，就是他們有一座小小的獻祭島，這很棒。

Content: Cakes, in a U. But I was still worried about like if you put this into game camera mode, game layout mode,
Content_zh: 蛋糕排成 U 字形。但我還是擔心，如果把這個放進遊戲鏡頭模式、遊戲版面模式，

Content: that'd be kind of blocked up.
Content_zh: 畫面會有點被擋住。

Content: And I did this real rough thing.
Content_zh: 所以我做了一個很粗略的版本。

Content: I just got Peter's revision, right.
Content_zh: 我剛拿到 Peter 的修改版，對。

Content: And so this was the idea like from a game camera you would be, you would kind of start here.
Content_zh: 所以概念是從遊戲鏡頭來看，你大概會從這裡開始。

Content: So we could start with a pan and we kind of move up and we kind of…
Content_zh: 我們可以從一個平移鏡頭開始，然後往上移，接著……

Content: Rice: But yeah, it's the scene where the main character, I mean main female character is being offered up for sacrifice to a monster.
Content_zh: Rice：對，這是主角，我是說女主角，被獻給怪物當祭品的場景。

Content: Schafer: Could it show up just behind the princess?
Content_zh: Schafer：它能不能就出現在公主後面？

Content: You just see something like feet?
Content_zh: 只看到像腳之類的東西？

Content: Like what the fuck is it!? Petty: Yeah.
Content_zh: 像是「那到底是什麼鬼！？」Petty：對。

Content: Schafer: You can't see it all, then it comes between, like oh my God!
Content_zh: Schafer：你看不到全貌，然後它插進中間，像是「我的天啊！」

Content: And it's gone.
Content_zh: 然後就不見了。

Content: You just never see it up close until it gets, comes in here and like eats that one.
Content_zh: 你一直不會近距離看到它，直到它進到這裡，像是把那個吃掉。

Content: Rice: Yeah, it's going to be a lot of work, for sure, because it's just a lot of animation and cut scenes.
Content_zh: Rice：對，這肯定會很費工，因為動畫和過場動畫真的很多。

Content: So it's going to be a tough one.
Content_zh: 所以這會很硬。

Content: Stapley: So you're saying for the end of this sprint we're really going to try to get the, get the sacrifice feast all set up?
Content_zh: Stapley：所以你的意思是，這個 sprint 結束前，我們真的要試著把獻祭宴會整個架起來？

Content: 'Cause that's the thing, like if every puzzle's going to have like say five or so new characters, that's like, kind of a lot of time.
Content_zh: 因為問題就在這裡，如果每個謎題都會有大概五個新角色，那會花很多時間。

Content: Petty: Well, and that's exactly why we want to take this to finals, because it's just conjecture
Content_zh: Petty：對，這正是我們想把這個做到最終品質的原因，因為在那之前都只是猜測，

Content: until we kind of get an area to really know exactly how long.
Content_zh: 直到我們真的做出一個區域，才會知道到底要花多久。

Content: I made estimates based on our progress.
Content_zh: 我根據我們的進度做了估算。

Content: Petty: Like there's always this period that when you start any game where you're trying to, you're trying to build it,
Content_zh: Petty：任何遊戲剛開始時總有這樣一段時間，你試著把它做出來，

Content: you're trying to build something both at like artistically you're like yeah, it's the right mood, that's the right game play, that's the right look.
Content_zh: 你試著做出某種東西，在美術上會覺得，對，這是正確的氣氛、正確的玩法、正確的樣子。

Content: But you're also trying to build something to get an idea of how long it takes you to build the rest of the game.
Content_zh: 但你也在做一個東西，好讓自己知道做完剩下的遊戲需要多久。

Content: And part of that is you need, you need enough information about the rest of the game,
Content_zh: 其中一部分是，你需要對遊戲其他部分有足夠資訊。

Content: like you know, there's always this theoretical ideal that you never get.
Content_zh: 總是會有那種理論上很理想、但你永遠拿不到的狀態。

Content: Like the whole game is written, every puzzle's designed, you know, all the art direction's completely done.
Content_zh: 像是整款遊戲都寫好了，每個謎題都設計好了，所有美術方向也都完全定案。

Content: I've never in my whatever years I've worked in the game industry, ever seen that happen.
Content_zh: 我在遊戲業做了這麼多年，從來沒看過那種事發生。

Content: Some projects are closer to that than others.
Content_zh: 有些專案會比較接近那種狀態。

Content: So there's a lot of uncertainty at that point.
Content_zh: 所以那個階段會有很多不確定性。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, sounds good and scary.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，聽起來又好又可怕。

Content: Schafer: Bagel, why you so scared?
Content_zh: Schafer：Bagel，你幹嘛這麼怕？

Content: Stapley: I think because I haven't seen anything like in the game yet, you know.
Content_zh: Stapley：我想是因為我還沒在遊戲裡看到什麼東西。

Content: Like it's still the lumberjack dude.
Content_zh: 現在還是那個伐木工老兄。

Content: I'd just love to see how easy it's going to be reshape things and put things in and take things out and change things and add things.
Content_zh: 我只是很想看看重塑東西、放東西進去、拿東西出來、修改和新增會有多容易。

Content: Petty: What you really have to do, I think, is lay the best foundation you can.
Content_zh: Petty：我覺得你真正要做的，是盡可能打好基礎。

Content: Be mindful of what you're making and then just be clever as you're making the game.
Content_zh: 清楚知道自己在做什麼，然後在做遊戲的過程中聰明應變。

Content: Look for opportunities to save time or to reuse things or to go in new directions that are just more efficient
Content_zh: 尋找可以省時間、重複利用，或是走向更有效率新方向的機會，

Content: or take some of the lessons you've learned from the earlier areas and adapt your later areas to it.
Content_zh: 或把前面區域學到的經驗，套用到後面的區域。

Content: So, that's, and that's, that's what I try and do and I think most people on the team try to do it in any game.
Content_zh: 所以那就是我會努力做的事，我想團隊裡大多數人在任何遊戲裡也都會這麼做。

Content: [Music] Offscreen Voice: She wants to be skinny, right?
Content_zh: [音樂] 畫外音：她想變瘦，對吧？

Content: And look skinny.
Content_zh: 看起來很瘦。

Content: So maybe it's something that helps her look slimmer sometime?
Content_zh: 所以也許是某種能讓她看起來更苗條的東西？

Content: Schafer: Corset. Offscreen Voice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：馬甲。畫外音：對。

Content: Schafer: But you put corset on the buzzard.
Content_zh: Schafer：但你把馬甲穿在禿鷹身上。

Content: Oh, wait. That could be awesome.
Content_zh: 喔，等等，那可能很棒。

Content: Offscreen Voice: That could be totally awesome.
Content_zh: 畫外音：那可能超棒。

Content: Schafer: So strange. A corset buzzard with a drumstick in his arm.
Content_zh: Schafer：太怪了。一隻穿馬甲、手上拿雞腿的禿鷹。

Content: This is where I start getting interested.
Content_zh: 這就是我開始感興趣的地方。

Content: Schafer: Like I haven't seen like the maiden feast thing all wired up yet, but that is in the game, I hear.
Content_zh: Schafer：我還沒看到 Maiden’s Feast 那段全都接起來，但我聽說它已經在遊戲裡了。

Content: Have you seen that?
Content_zh: 你看過了嗎？

Content: I've been out of it in meetings.
Content_zh: 我一直在開會，完全沒跟上。

Content: Things are happening so fast.
Content_zh: 事情進展太快了。

Content: Petty: You know what did so far in it, is you know, we had this sort of layout drawing that we did.
Content_zh: Petty：目前我們做了什麼呢？我們有之前畫的那種版面草圖。

Content: And we took those layout drawings and cut them up.
Content_zh: 我們把那些版面草圖切開。

Content: And you can sort of see things exist in the tool here in parallax.
Content_zh: 你可以在工具裡看到這些東西以視差方式存在。

Content: We don't have the game camera authored or anything, like you know, you could rough in the game play right now if you wanted to.
Content_zh: 我們還沒有正式設定遊戲鏡頭之類的，但如果你想，現在就可以先粗略做出玩法。

Content: This isn't the, a real monster design.
Content_zh: 這還不是真正的怪物設計。

Content: That's not a real bird design.
Content_zh: 那也不是真正的鳥類設計。

Content: Those aren't real character designs, but they are, you know, they have the underlying script components to them.
Content_zh: 那些也不是真正的角色設計，但它們底下已經有腳本元件。

Content: And so, you know, once those, you can sort of see here this is just, if we run the game at this scene at the moment
Content_zh: 所以你可以在這裡看到，如果我們現在執行這個場景，

Content: we don't actually have a main character.
Content_zh: 其實還沒有主角。

Content: So you know, one could hookup dialogue and have proxy object dragging and all that sort of stuff.
Content_zh: 所以我們可以接上對白，做代理物件拖曳之類的東西。

Content: So that's the biggest thing I want to do with it, was put enough of it together that Bagel could do some painting and learn from the painting.
Content_zh: 我最想做的就是把它組到夠完整，讓 Bagel 可以畫一些圖，並從繪製過程中學到東西。

Content: And if the programmers want to script that one first they can.
Content_zh: 如果程式設計師想先替那段寫腳本，也可以。

Content: Franzke: Stuff should go time-wise like, well, at least roughly, right, you need those animations
Content_zh: Franzke：時間安排上應該要，至少大致上對吧，你需要那些動畫。

Content: Kipnis: Think of the sound guys, that's what they use as well.
Content_zh: Kipnis：想想音效人員，他們也會用到那個。

Content: Petty: Well, this happened but their workflow, all they do, is they take…
Content_zh: Petty：嗯，這是發生了，但他們的工作流程就是拿……

Content: Kipnis: Oh! [Power out] Schafer: Well, it is lunchtime.
Content_zh: Kipnis：喔！[停電] Schafer：好吧，也到午餐時間了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: It could have been worse.
Content_zh: 畫外音：情況可能更糟。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Do you see yourself having a huge workload on this?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你覺得自己在這上面會有很大的工作量嗎？

Content: Crook: Yes. Yeah, we started going through it and I figure, I mean it's a Tim Schafer game.
Content_zh: Crook：會。對，我們開始看過一遍，我想，畢竟這是 Tim Schafer 的遊戲。

Content: He likes characters, he likes dialogue.
Content_zh: 他喜歡角色，也喜歡對白。

Content: And there'll be cut scenes.
Content_zh: 而且會有過場動畫。

Content: Crook: I don't know.
Content_zh: Crook：我不知道。

Content: I'm not sure what Tim wants as far as fidelity on cut scenes.
Content_zh: 我不確定 Tim 對過場動畫的精細度想要到什麼程度。

Content: Like I don't know.
Content_zh: 我真的不知道。

Content: Petty: The question is how Full Throttle are we?
Content_zh: Petty：問題是我們要做到多《Full Throttle》？

Content: Crook: Right. Right.
Content_zh: Crook：對，對。

Content: For an animator this is horrible. But, Franzke: I mean, like what if you could just do all that stuff in Maya
Content_zh: 對動畫師來說這很可怕。不過，Franzke：我是說，如果那些東西都能直接在 Maya 裡做呢？

Content: Crook: We'll see how much of it we can auto-gen as far as like lip-sync and that sort of thing.
Content_zh: Crook：我們會看看像對嘴之類的東西，有多少可以自動產生。

Content: I just, I just hope, you know, sometimes you do something, you think, oh, we'll just automate it.
Content_zh: 我只是希望，因為有時候你做某件事，會想說，喔，我們把它自動化就好。

Content: And then you do it and like ah, it's not going to work.
Content_zh: 結果真的做了才發現，啊，這行不通。

Content: You know, we need to just have, you know, artists go in and do this.
Content_zh: 我們還是得讓美術進去手動處理。

Content: Crook: So this was sort of a test animation that I did yesterday.
Content_zh: Crook：這算是我昨天做的測試動畫。

Content: And so she's stuck in this cake.
Content_zh: 她卡在這個蛋糕裡。

Content: And then this just shows you her turning, just to see what kind of, if we can get a quick turn out of her.
Content_zh: 這裡只是讓你看到她轉身，看看能不能讓她快速轉一下。

Content: And so we already have the composition decided on so we know what the position of each of these characters will be in.
Content_zh: 我們已經決定構圖了，所以知道每個角色會在什麼位置。

Content: So when Bagel did the initial designs, which is here, he took that into consideration.
Content_zh: 所以 Bagel 在做這裡的初步設計時，已經把這點考慮進去了。

Content: And so we know that she will never have to turn any more than what she does right there.
Content_zh: 因此我們知道，她轉身的幅度不會比這裡更多。

Content: Rice: Just because that's kind of a whole new process, the 2D animation is something we haven't done here at Double Fine.
Content_zh: Rice：因為那算是全新的流程，2D 動畫是我們在 Double Fine 還沒做過的事。

Content: And we're doing it in a way that is similar to how we animate in 3D so that helps.
Content_zh: 而我們用的方式跟 3D 動畫有點類似，所以有幫助。

Content: But that could easily be something that's a lot more work than we're expecting.
Content_zh: 但那很可能會比我們預期多出很多工作。

Content: Petty: Yeah, I'm not sure how we're going to handle the tentacles because when we talked about maybe doing them purely as flip book animation,
Content_zh: Petty：對，我不確定我們要怎麼處理觸手，因為我們有談過也許純粹用逐格動畫來做，

Content: because we are, we don't have, we can't have enough joints in this engine to do, to like animate them all freely.
Content_zh: 因為這個引擎沒有辦法放足夠的骨節，讓所有觸手都自由動畫化。

Content: Schafer: Is there a special close-up monster different than the...?
Content_zh: Schafer：特寫用的怪物會跟那個不一樣嗎……？

Content: Petty: We don't do any special close-up.
Content_zh: Petty：我們不做特別的特寫版本。

Content: We do one version for everything unless we have to do it, because it's just a lot more work.
Content_zh: 除非非做不可，不然所有東西都只做一個版本，因為那會多很多工作。

Content: Schafer: Maybe not to joint everything that you don't want to joint.
Content_zh: Schafer：也許不用把不想上骨節的東西都上骨節。

Content: Or is it the painting of the details or is the mouth shapes and all that stuff?
Content_zh: 還是問題在細節繪製、嘴型之類的東西？

Content: Petty: All the above.
Content_zh: Petty：以上皆是。

Content: Rice: We're still working on like our first playable puzzle from the actual game.
Content_zh: Rice：我們還在做實際遊戲裡的第一個可玩謎題。

Content: We kind of bit off a big one.
Content_zh: 我們有點挑了個太大的。

Content: So that's happening now.
Content_zh: 現在正在處理這個。

Content: Crook: So you know, when I was creating lists and I've been working with Lee on schedule
Content_zh: Crook：我在建立清單，也一直跟 Lee 一起排時程，

Content: and it's, it's, it's going to get hairy, I think.
Content_zh: 我覺得事情會變得很棘手。

Content: Petty: And then his overall layout of that area, I think this shows, this shows where it is relative to the village.
Content_zh: Petty：然後這是他對那個區域的整體配置，我想這張圖顯示了它相對於村莊的位置。

Content: Schafer: Things look great.
Content_zh: Schafer：看起來很棒。

Content: Schafer: So we'll meet with Peter.
Content_zh: Schafer：那我們就跟 Peter 開會。

Content: Chan: This is kind of the square, but I, you know, it could be a lot bigger, but I wanted to just show what it might look like down the street.
Content_zh: Chan：這裡有點像廣場，不過它可以大很多，我只是想先展示從街道往下看可能會是什麼樣子。

Content: Schafer: I guess I'd like, I haven't seen any, I mean, I like the...
Content_zh: Schafer：我想我會想要……我還沒看到任何……我是說，我喜歡這個……

Content: Schafer: Yeah, some things are bugging me about those mushroom houses.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，那些蘑菇屋有些地方讓我在意。

Content: For one, I hate mushrooms.
Content_zh: 第一，我討厭蘑菇。

Content: But two, it's like, oh, mushroom houses.
Content_zh: 第二，感覺就是，喔，蘑菇屋。

Content: What is that making me think of?
Content_zh: 那會讓我想到什麼？

Content: And I thought Smurfs.
Content_zh: 我想到藍精靈。

Content: Schafer: You know, my only worry about the mushroom town is if it invokes Smurfdom, you know, because there is that kind of Smurf shape with mushrooms a little bit.
Content_zh: Schafer：我對蘑菇鎮唯一的擔心，是它會不會讓人聯想到藍精靈，因為蘑菇多少會有那種藍精靈的形狀。

Content: So when I see the big castle's one big mushroom, you know, there's parts, there's shapes of mushrooms that don't evoke that and there's shapes that do.
Content_zh: 所以當我看到大城堡是一朵大蘑菇時，蘑菇有些部位和形狀不會讓人想到那個，有些則會。

Content: Petty: Like this doesn't really, right? Chan: OK.
Content_zh: Petty：像這個其實不太會，對吧？Chan：好。

Content: Schafer: No, that looks more like a…
Content_zh: Schafer：不，那看起來比較像……

Content: Chan: Greg? When is my last day?
Content_zh: Chan：Greg？我最後一天是什麼時候？

Content: Petty: August tenth. August tenth.
Content_zh: Petty：八月十號，八月十號。

Content: Chan: Wow. OK.
Content_zh: Chan：哇，好。

Content: So then we are, it's... Rice: Getting there.
Content_zh: 那我們就是，這……Rice：快到了。

Content: Chan: We're running out of time.
Content_zh: Chan：我們快沒時間了。

Content: Schafer: Get drawing right now.
Content_zh: Schafer：現在馬上去畫。

Content: Chan: We've only just scratched the surface really, by that. We're still...
Content_zh: Chan：那樣算起來，我們真的才剛碰到表面而已。我們還……

Content: Schafer: We'll get more money later and have you back.
Content_zh: Schafer：之後我們會弄到更多錢，再請你回來。

Content: Rice: You know, we got a lot of money but that money goes a lot of places and it's expensive to run a studio like Double Fine in San Francisco.
Content_zh: Rice：我們是拿到很多錢，但那些錢要花在很多地方，而且在舊金山經營 Double Fine 這樣的工作室很貴。

Content: So there's only so long we can have this many people on the project without running out of money.
Content_zh: 所以我們能讓這麼多人待在專案上的時間有限，不然錢就會花完。

Content: We've got to make sure that we make something great in that amount of time.
Content_zh: 我們必須確保在那段時間內做出很棒的東西。

Content: Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。

Content: Chan: Anything else? Schafer: Not me.
Content_zh: Chan：還有別的嗎？Schafer：我沒有。

Content: Petty: No, no. I'll call you soon.
Content_zh: Petty：沒有，沒有。我很快再打給你。

Content: Chan: OK, cool. I'm going to hang up.
Content_zh: Chan：好，酷。那我要掛了。

Content: You guys have a great rest of the day.
Content_zh: 祝你們接下來今天過得愉快。

Content: Schafer: Thanks, you too Peter. Bye.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝，你也是，Peter。拜。

Content: Chan: OK, thank you, bye-bye.
Content_zh: Chan：好，謝謝，拜拜。

Content: Schafer: So like I really do like all this stuff, and this stuff.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我是真的很喜歡這些東西，還有這些。

Content: And that all works, too.
Content_zh: 那些也都行得通。

Content: Like I was, I hope I didn't hurt his feelings by saying Smurfs.
Content_zh: 我只是希望我說藍精靈沒有傷到他的感情。

Content: Sometimes you never know...Smurfs..
Content_zh: 有時候你真的很難知道……藍精靈……

Content: Petty: It's really hard not to offend artists all the time.
Content_zh: Petty：要一直不冒犯藝術家真的很難。

Content: Schafer: But that's my only worry about some of this stuff.
Content_zh: Schafer：但那是我對這些東西唯一的擔心。

Content: Because I, I mean, I like it but he did go, like the very first thing that he ever talked about
Content_zh: 因為我是說，我喜歡它，但他一開始談到的第一件事，

Content: was this Japanese/Scandinavian combination mushroom thing.
Content_zh: 就是這種日本加斯堪地那維亞風格的蘑菇東西。

Content: And so it's really hard to say like I like it, but I had seen anything else, except I did also like
Content_zh: 所以很難說「我喜歡」，但我也沒看過其他東西，不過我也喜歡

Content: some of Bagel's crazy geometric weird thing that he was doing, too.
Content_zh: Bagel 當時做的一些瘋狂幾何怪東西。

Content: That was kind of a unique look for the castle.
Content_zh: 那種城堡外觀很獨特。

Content: Yeah, I mean I know Bagel's got a lot of backgrounds to do but I would like to see if he could come up with any ideas about the buzzard.
Content_zh: 對，我知道 Bagel 有很多背景要畫，但我想看看他能不能對禿鷹想出什麼點子。

Content: Petty: So is Bagel, yeah, I mean, so what we have to do, like with, I guess we can talk about this in the reviews or review meeting,
Content_zh: Petty：所以 Bagel 也是，對，我是說，我們得做的是……我想我們可以在 review 或 review 會議裡談，

Content: but Bagel right now, just, just to paint what we already designed for just the sacrifice puzzle will take him this entire sprint.
Content_zh: 但 Bagel 現在光是把我們已經為獻祭謎題設計好的東西畫完，就會花掉他整個 sprint。

Content: He doesn't, he really doesn't have time for, for any concept art at all.
Content_zh: 他真的完全沒有時間做任何概念美術。

Content: He has time to interpret it like when he gets to like paint it and that's sort of built into the time.
Content_zh: 他有時間在實際繪製時詮釋它，而那算是已經包含在時程裡。

Content: And if, we can certainly do any of that but it comes at the cost of we either need to come up with another solution for painting that-
Content_zh: 我們當然可以做那些事，但代價是我們得想出另一個繪製方案，或者……

Content: Schafer: The problem with that is that doesn't really solve the problem of like having a game be in Bagel's style.
Content_zh: Schafer：那樣的問題是，它其實沒有解決讓遊戲呈現 Bagel 風格這件事。

Content: 'Cause if he isn't doing the concept part of it, then it's not really, Petty: well I dissagree... Schafer: it's his rendering style but it's not his like...
Content_zh: 因為如果概念部分不是他做的，那就不是真的……Petty：嗯，我不同意……Schafer：那只是他的渲染風格，但不是他的那種……

Content: Petty: But that's what his painting is.
Content_zh: Petty：但他的繪畫就是那樣啊。

Content: You're not using the content from his painting.
Content_zh: 你不是在用他畫裡的內容。

Content: You're not using the "Bob's Big Boy" Schafer: When I talked about like wanting to capture
Content_zh: 你不是在用「Bob's Big Boy」那種東西。Schafer：我說想捕捉

Content: some of the crazy stuff from his fine art show, there was a little guy on the table or the woman with the mouth in her hair.
Content_zh: 他純藝術展裡那些瘋狂東西時，像是桌上的小人，或頭髮裡有嘴巴的女人。

Content: Like those are more ideas than about a rendering style.
Content_zh: 那些比較是點子，而不只是渲染風格。

Content: So like I wanted to capture some of Bagel's, some more surreal ideas in the game concept, too.
Content_zh: 所以我也想在遊戲概念裡捕捉一些 Bagel 更超現實的想法。

Content: Not just, not just his rendering style, for sure.
Content_zh: 絕對不只是他的渲染風格。

Content: Petty: Well then we need to come up with another plan, because right now it's not possible.
Content_zh: Petty：那我們就得想出另一個計畫，因為現在這樣不可能。

Content: Schafer: But, 'cause I'm torn.
Content_zh: Schafer：可是，因為我很兩難。

Content: Because I also want Bagel to do concepts, like come up with crazy ideas.
Content_zh: 因為我也希望 Bagel 做概念，想出那些瘋狂點子。

Content: That's one of the things I like about his art, is that he has these, you know, kind of crazy surreal ideas in his painting.
Content_zh: 我喜歡他的藝術，其中一點就是他的畫裡有那些瘋狂又超現實的想法。

Content: Not just the rendering style of it.
Content_zh: 不只是它的渲染風格。

Content: Rice: But yeah, I mean it was a hard choice, you know, when Tim decided that he wanted the game to reflect Bagel's style.
Content_zh: Rice：但對，Tim 決定希望遊戲反映 Bagel 的風格時，那是個艱難的選擇。

Content: Ultimately that means that he's going to have to paint a lot of the game, if not all of the game.
Content_zh: 最終那代表他必須畫遊戲很大一部分，甚至可能是全部。

Content: And it's going to be what makes the game have kind of a cohesive feel and look.
Content_zh: 也正是這點會讓遊戲有一致的感覺和外觀。

Content: Petty: I don't see how it's going to work.
Content_zh: Petty：我看不出這要怎麼行得通。

Content: So we could either do something like, you know, someone else could render the characters
Content_zh: 所以我們可以做某種安排，像是讓別人去渲染角色，

Content: to give him time to do concept design or someone, I don't know.
Content_zh: 讓他有時間做概念設計，或找別人，我不知道。

Content: Something like that, some hybrid sort of rendering style solution that can work in a very similar style.
Content_zh: 類似那樣，用某種混合式的渲染風格方案，做出非常接近的風格。

Content: That's the only, you know, we'll have to figure something like that out.
Content_zh: 這是唯一的……我們得想出類似的辦法。

Content: Because there just isn't time for one person to do all that.
Content_zh: 因為一個人根本沒有時間做完那些。

Content: Schafer: I love this meeting wherein we talk about can't be done.
Content_zh: Schafer：我真喜歡這種專門討論做不到的會議。

Content: Because those are always like, those are kind of a drag, 'cause you're like trying, you're trying to invent stuff.
Content_zh: 因為那種會議總是有點掃興，畢竟你正在努力發明東西。

Content: You know, you're having this problem with nothing to something, like OK, I got an idea.
Content_zh: 你是在面對從無到有的問題，像是，好，我有個點子。

Content: And someone says that'll take three years; you can't do it.
Content_zh: 然後有人說那要花三年，你做不到。

Content: And you're like oh.
Content_zh: 你就只能說，喔。

Content: Do you know how hard that was to have that idea?
Content_zh: 你知道想出那個點子有多難嗎？

Content: Schafer: OK, I have to go do something.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，我得去做點事了。

Content: Petty: OK. So...
Content_zh: Petty：好，所以……

Content: Schafer: As much as I would like to talk about how we can't make this awesome game in time.
Content_zh: Schafer：雖然我很想繼續聊我們怎麼沒辦法準時做出這款超棒的遊戲。

Content: Petty: I thought I was coming up with ways that we could.
Content_zh: Petty：我以為我是在想出我們做得到的方法。

Content: Schafer: I know. I'm going to check out that inventory stuff.
Content_zh: Schafer：我知道。我要去看看那些 inventory 的東西。

Content: Rice: Bye.
Content_zh: Rice：拜。

Content: Schafer: Hey, Anna. Kipnis: Yeah?
Content_zh: Schafer：嘿，Anna。Kipnis：嗯？

Content: Can I see all that cool inventory stuff I missed?
Content_zh: 我可以看看我錯過的那些酷 inventory 東西嗎？

Content: Petty: We're just going to wind up shipping a game about a red bot and a fucking lumberjack.
Content_zh: Petty：到最後我們只會推出一款關於紅色機器人和該死伐木工的遊戲。

Content: Sorry, Tim.
Content_zh: 抱歉，Tim。

Content: We decided to ship these instead.
Content_zh: 我們決定改推出這些。

Content: Crook: Ran out of time.
Content_zh: Crook：沒時間了。

Content: Franzke: We ran out of time. We did create all kind of tech for it...
Content_zh: Franzke：我們沒時間了。不過我們確實為它做了各種技術……

Content: Thank God it's up to you to make a game.
Content_zh: 還好做遊戲是你的事。

Content: Here's a tool set, go do whatever you want.
Content_zh: 工具組在這，想做什麼都行。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I think there's always a point in a project where everything kind of gets real and you see the amount of work
Content_zh: Rice：對，我覺得每個專案總會有一刻，一切突然變得很真實，你看見眼前的工作量，

Content: that you have in front of you.
Content_zh: 就在你面前。

Content: There was a bit of an eye-opener just through the docs and the processes as far as how much work is left.
Content_zh: 光是看文件和流程，就很清楚意識到還剩多少工作。

Content: And how much we can, we can do.
Content_zh: 以及我們能做多少。

Content: And the amount of time we have left.
Content_zh: 還有剩下多少時間。

Content: Rice: It was an eye-opener that we needed to get to the moment where we're like OK, we need to start building this game.
Content_zh: Rice：那讓我們醒悟，必須走到這一刻，說好，我們得開始把這款遊戲做出來。

Content: And we're getting to the point where yeah, where now the real work is beginning.
Content_zh: 而我們正在抵達那個階段，現在真正的工作開始了。

Content: Schafer: Who was it, it was like Miyamoto.
Content_zh: Schafer：那是誰說的，好像是宮本茂。

Content: He said that famous quote about like it only hurts until it's done, but if it sucks, it sucks forever, you know.
Content_zh: 他有句名言，大意是痛苦只會持續到完成為止，但如果東西很爛，它就會永遠爛下去。

Content: Schafer: So like OK, we'll suffer now, we'll put the extra work in to make this great,
Content_zh: Schafer：所以好吧，我們現在吃點苦，多投入一點工作，把它做得很棒，

Content: because no one's going to remember, you know, no one ever says about a game like, oh you know, that game had a really reasonable production cycle.
Content_zh: 因為沒有人會記得，也沒有人會稱讚一款遊戲說，喔，那款遊戲的製作週期真的很合理。

Content: That was, I really enjoyed that game.
Content_zh: 所以我真的很喜歡那款遊戲。

Content: It was really moderately scheduled.
Content_zh: 它的時程安排得真是中規中矩。

Content: Rice: This was our fourth milestone of 13.
Content_zh: Rice：這是我們十三個里程碑中的第四個。

Content: So we need to start kind of pushing forward, getting some content together if we're going to stay on track.
Content_zh: 所以如果我們要保持進度，就得開始往前推，把一些內容做出來。

Content: Schafer: I mean, off of what I'm pushing for is like we should just think about this problem longer,
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，我主張的是我們應該多想一下這個問題，

Content: because we're going to have a creative solution that's going to allow us to have the great thing that I want,
Content_zh: 因為我們會想出一個有創意的解法，讓我們得到我想要的那個很棒的東西，

Content: but have it not take 20 years to put together.
Content_zh: 但又不需要花二十年才能做出來。

Content: It'll take, we'll think of the cheap.
Content_zh: 我們會想出便宜的做法。

Content: You know, Lee and those guys are really smart and they could probably think of a clever way to, you know,
Content_zh: Lee 他們都很聰明，應該可以想出聰明的方法，

Content: cut the work in half and get it done.
Content_zh: 把工作量砍半，然後完成它。

Content: Schafer: But it's hard to commit to that in advance.
Content_zh: Schafer：但很難事先承諾一定做得到。

Content: Like OK, I'll have a clever idea and it'll cut this work in half, 'cause you know, those things you don't know they're going to come.
Content_zh: 像是說好，我會想出聰明點子，把工作量砍半，因為那種東西你不知道它會不會出現。

Content: But they always come.
Content_zh: 但它們總是會出現。

Content: [Music] Offscreen Voice: Bye, guys. See you tomorrow.
Content_zh: [音樂] 畫外音：拜，各位，明天見。

Content: [Music] Rice: Potentially getting some art interns from the Institute.
Content_zh: [音樂] Rice：也許可以從 Institute 找一些美術實習生。

Content: That could do, you know, like the menial tasks nobody wants to do.
Content_zh: 可以做那些沒人想做的雜務。

Content: Franzke: Well not even menial, like, I mean all of my plans...
Content_zh: Franzke：也不一定是雜務，我是說，我所有的計畫……
